name: Overnight Oats
description: Creamy no-cook oats prepped the night before, topped with banana and honey.
instructions: Combine oats, milk, greek yogurt, and chia seeds in a jar or container.
Stir well, cover, and refrigerate overnight (at least 6 hours).
In the morning, slice the banana and arrange on top.
Drizzle with honey and serve cold.

name: Greek Yogurt Parfait
description: Layers of thick Greek yogurt, fresh berries, and crunchy granola for a quick, high-protein breakfast.
instructions: Spoon half the greek yogurt into the bottom of a glass or bowl.
Add half the berries, then half the granola.
Repeat layers with remaining yogurt, berries, and granola.
Drizzle with honey and serve immediately.

name: Scrambled Eggs with Toast
description: Fluffy scrambled eggs with buttered whole-wheat toast — a fast, filling breakfast staple.
instructions: Crack eggs into a bowl, add milk, salt, and pepper; whisk until uniform.
Melt butter in a non-stick skillet over medium-low heat.
Pour in egg mixture; cook, gently folding with a spatula, until just set (about 3 minutes).
Toast the bread and butter lightly.
Serve eggs alongside toast.

name: Banana Protein Pancakes
description: Three-ingredient pancakes made with banana, egg, and protein powder — great for meal prep.
instructions: Mash bananas in a bowl until smooth.
Add eggs, protein powder, and baking powder; mix until a batter forms.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat and grease lightly with coconut oil.
Pour 1/4 cup batter per pancake; cook 2–3 minutes until bubbles form, then flip and cook 1–2 minutes more.
Serve with maple syrup.

name: Avocado Toast with Egg
description: Smashed avocado on toasted sourdough topped with a fried egg and red pepper flakes.
instructions: Toast sourdough slices until golden and crisp.
Halve the avocado, remove the pit, and scoop flesh into a bowl.
Add lemon juice and salt; mash to desired consistency.
Spread avocado evenly over toast.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat; fry the egg sunny-side up, about 3 minutes.
Place egg on top of the toast and sprinkle with red pepper flakes.

name: Grilled Chicken Salad
description: Tender grilled chicken breast over mixed greens with cherry tomatoes and a light vinaigrette.
instructions: Season chicken breast with salt and pepper.
Grill or pan-sear over medium-high heat for 6–7 minutes per side until cooked through (internal temp 165°F).
Let rest 5 minutes, then slice.
In a large bowl, combine greens, halved cherry tomatoes, and sliced cucumber.
Whisk together olive oil and red wine vinegar for the dressing; toss with salad.
Top with sliced chicken and serve.

name: Turkey and Avocado Wrap
description: Deli turkey, creamy avocado, and crisp vegetables wrapped in a whole-wheat tortilla.
instructions: Lay the tortilla flat on a clean surface.
Spread dijon mustard across the center.
Slice avocado and lay across the tortilla.
Layer turkey, lettuce, and sliced tomato on top.
Fold in sides and roll tightly from bottom to top.
Slice in half on the diagonal and serve.

name: Quinoa Buddha Bowl
description: Hearty grain bowl with roasted sweet potato, chickpea, and tahini dressing over fluffy quinoa.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F. Cube sweet potato, toss with olive oil and salt, roast 25 minutes until tender.
Cook quinoa per package directions (typically 2:1 water ratio, 15 minutes).
Drain and rinse chickpeas; toss with garlic powder and a drizzle of olive oil.
Whisk together tahini, lemon juice, and 2 tbsp water to make dressing; season with salt.
Divide quinoa into bowls; top with roasted sweet potato, chickpeas, and baby spinach.
Drizzle with tahini dressing and serve.

name: Tuna Salad Sandwich
description: Classic protein-packed tuna salad with celery and light mayo on whole-wheat bread.
instructions: Drain tuna well and flake into a mixing bowl.
Dice celery and red onion; add to bowl.
Mix in mayonnaise and dijon mustard; season with black pepper.
Toast bread if desired.
Layer lettuce on one slice of bread, spoon tuna mixture on top, close sandwich.
Serve immediately or refrigerate tuna mix up to 2 days.

name: Red Lentil Soup
description: Warming one-pot red lentil soup with cumin and tomato — great for meal prep.
instructions: Dice onion and carrots; mince garlic.
Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat; sauté onion and carrots 5 minutes until softened.
Add garlic and cumin; cook 1 minute until fragrant.
Rinse lentils and add to pot along with diced tomatoes and chicken broth.
Bring to a boil, reduce heat, and simmer 20 minutes until lentils are completely soft.
Use an immersion blender to partially blend (leave some texture); season with salt.

name: Baked Salmon with Roasted Vegetables
description: Herb-crusted salmon fillet baked alongside seasonal vegetables for a complete, easy dinner.
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F. Line a baking sheet with foil.
Chop broccoli, bell pepper, and zucchini into bite-sized pieces; toss with 1 tbsp olive oil, salt, and pepper.
Spread vegetables on half the baking sheet and roast 10 minutes.
Place salmon on the other half of the sheet; drizzle with remaining olive oil, garlic powder, dill, and lemon juice.
Roast 12–15 minutes until salmon flakes easily and vegetables are tender.
Serve with lemon wedges.

name: Chicken and Vegetable Stir Fry
description: Quick weeknight stir fry with chicken, colorful vegetables, and a savory ginger-soy sauce over rice.
instructions: Cook rice per package directions.
Slice chicken into thin strips; toss with cornstarch, a pinch of salt.
Whisk together soy sauce, sesame oil, minced garlic, and grated ginger for the sauce.
Heat vegetable oil in a wok or large skillet over high heat; cook chicken 5–6 minutes until golden; remove.
Add broccoli, snap peas, and sliced carrots to the pan; stir fry 4–5 minutes.
Return chicken; pour sauce over everything; toss and cook 1 minute until sauce thickens.
Serve over steamed rice.

name: Turkey Meatballs with Pasta
description: Lean ground turkey meatballs in a simple marinara sauce served over whole-wheat spaghetti.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F.
Mince garlic; combine with turkey, egg, breadcrumbs, parmesan, oregano, and salt; mix until just combined.
Form into 16 meatballs (about 1.5 inch diameter); place on an oiled baking sheet.
Bake 18–20 minutes until cooked through (internal temp 165°F).
Warm marinara sauce in a saucepan; add meatballs and simmer 5 minutes.
Meanwhile, cook spaghetti per package directions; drain.
Serve meatballs and sauce over pasta; top with extra parmesan if desired.

name: Black Bean Tacos
description: Smoky spiced black bean tacos with fresh pico de gallo and avocado — ready in 20 minutes.
instructions: Drain and rinse black beans; add to a skillet with olive oil, cumin, chili powder, and salt.
Cook over medium heat 5 minutes, mashing about half the beans for texture.
Dice tomato, red onion, and cilantro; toss with lime juice for a quick pico de gallo.
Warm tortillas in a dry skillet 30 seconds per side.
Slice avocado.
Assemble tacos: bean mixture, pico, and avocado slices; serve with remaining lime.

name: Sheet Pan Chicken and Sweet Potato
description: One-pan meal of seasoned chicken thighs and caramelized sweet potatoes — easy cleanup.
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F. Line a large baking sheet with foil.
Cube sweet potatoes and slice red onion into wedges; spread on the sheet.
Mix olive oil with smoked paprika, garlic powder, thyme, salt, and pepper.
Rub half the spiced oil over the vegetables and toss to coat.
Nestle chicken thighs skin-side up among the vegetables; brush with remaining spiced oil.
Roast 35–40 minutes until chicken skin is crispy and internal temp reaches 165°F.
Serve directly from the pan.

name: Apple with Peanut Butter
description: Crisp apple slices paired with natural peanut butter — a quick, balanced snack.
instructions: Wash and core the apple; slice into 8 wedges.
Serve alongside peanut butter for dipping.

name: Hard Boiled Eggs
description: Simple batch-cooked hard boiled eggs — the ultimate high-protein meal-prep snack.
instructions: Place eggs in a saucepan and cover with cold water by 1 inch.
Bring to a full boil over medium-high heat.
Remove from heat, cover, and let sit 10–11 minutes for hard boiled yolks.
Transfer to an ice bath for 5 minutes to stop cooking.
Peel and serve with a pinch of salt.

name: Cottage Cheese with Berries
description: High-protein cottage cheese topped with fresh berries and a drizzle of honey for a light snack.
instructions: Spoon cottage cheese into a bowl.
Top with mixed berries.
Drizzle with honey and serve immediately.
